slides.title: I Can Fix The World
slides.description: We spent our youth staring at blinking cursors, convinced they held the secrets to a better reality. But access remained just out of reach.

slides.title: Living In The Machine
slides.description: The transition from physical reality to the digital interface began in our bedrooms, where the glow of the cathode ray tube was our only window.

slides.title: The Promise of 90s Tech
slides.description: We were a generation raised on 14.4k modems, believing that interconnectedness would lead to a techno-utopia.

slides.title: Hacking The Threshold
slides.description: The line between the user and the system begins to blur as we prepare to push the boundaries of what is possible.

slides.title: Touching The Digital Divine
slides.description: Like Michelangelo’s fresco, the human spark finally connects with the algorithmic potential.

slides.title: The Portal Opens
slides.description: The barrier between the physical room and the digital infinite dissolves into a field of pure potential.

slides.title: Stepping Into The Green
slides.description: We leave the constraints of the 90s behind, entering a space where the digital and the biological are one.

slides.title: An Integrated Future
slides.description: We sit upon the hills of a new world, a landscape no longer rendered in wireframe, but flourishing in real time.

slides.title: The Mechanical Bloom
slides.description: Technology is no longer cold copper and silicon; it’s a living, breathing mechanism.

slides.title: The Infinite City
slides.description: Scalable, sustainable, and beautiful—the new urban center grows in harmony with the terrain.

slides.title: Speeding Through The Grid
slides.description: We navigate the veins of this living network with the speed and thrill of the early digital pioneers.

slides.title: Drifting Into The Night
slides.description: Even in the nocturnal hours, the network breathes with us, warm and responsive.

slides.title: AI for Everyone
slides.description: The complex power of the source code is finally distilled into a simple, beautiful, human-centric interface.

slides.title: Think Different — This Time With The Source Code
slides.description: We remember the past, but we look forward to a world where everyone creates the future.

transitions.prompt: The green text character by character shifts into the glowing computer monitors of the messy bedroom.
The cluster of flickering monitors zooms out to reveal the full wooden desk and the person holding the game controller.
The CRT screen on the desk pulses and expands, transforming the game into the 'Confirm' dialog box.
The glowing pixel hand on the screen stretches out and morphs into the dithered 'Creation of Adam' hand reaching for the window.
The 'PROJECT.EXE' window grows large and becomes the rectangular portal looking out into the green Windows-style landscape.
The camera moves through the portal, tilting upward from the tiled floor to the person sitting on the ceiling tiles looking at the sky.
The person on the ceiling drops down, landing softly as the scene seamlessly morphs into the friends sitting on the grassy hill.
The blue sky grid above the hill zooms in quickly, the tiles melting into the glass and moss-filled components of the PORSCHE watch.
The interior watch gears rotate to become the winding river and valley city skyline.
The river surface ripples and stretches forward, turning into the path through the crystalline archways as the dirt bike emerges.
The dirt bike continues forward, turning into the car driving through the glowing cyberpunk city at night.
The car dashboard lights defocus, rearranging their shapes into the buttons and screen of the SONY MG+ device.
The SONY device slides away, replaced by the retro computers of the final slide as the 'Think Different' headline fades in.
